title: Button
subtitle: Versatile button component with multiple variants, sizes and states

basic.title: Basic button
basic.description: The simplest form of a button with default styles.

variants-colors.title: Variants and colors
variants-colors.description: Supports different variants and colors from the NUI system.
variants-colors.note: Combine color and variant to customize the button appearance to your needs.

shapes.title: Shapes
shapes.description: Control the button shape with rounded border options.
shapes.note: Shape options: 'rounded' for slightly rounded borders, 'pill' for fully rounded borders, and 'square' for straight borders.

raised.title: Raised buttons
raised.description: Add visual depth to your buttons with a subtle shadow.
raised.note: Raised buttons are ideal for primary actions you want to highlight in your interface. Use the raised attribute on your Button component.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five size options available in NUI.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the button height, horizontal padding and font size. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg' and 'xl'.

icons.title: Icons
icons.description: Add icons to buttons using prefixIcon (before content) or suffixIcon (after). You can mix both for patterns like ← Prev / Next →.
icons.note: Icon-only buttons (only prefixIcon or suffixIcon, no text) are rendered as square/rounded icon buttons and require aria-label or title for accessibility.

loading.title: Loading state
loading.description: Displays a loading spinner in three positions: start, end or center (which replaces the content).
loading.note: Loading positions: 'start' shows the spinner before the text, 'end' shows it after, 'center' completely replaces the content with a centered spinner. If the button only contains an icon, the content is also replaced by the spinner while loading.

width.title: Width
width.description: Control the button width: auto (default), full (100%) or fit (adjusted to content).
width.note: Width options: 'auto' adapts to content with standard padding, 'full' stretches to 100% of the container, 'fit' uses the minimum required space.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Disabled buttons prevent user interaction and show a dimmed appearance.

events.title: Events
events.description: Handle click events with the onClick output. Supports button types: button, submit and reset.
events.consoleNote: Open DevTools (F12) and check the Console tab to see the events logged when you click on the buttons.

directive.title: Using directive
directive.description: Apply button styles to native HTML elements using the nuiButton directive.
directive.note: You can use the nuiButton directive on any native HTML element such as <button>, <a> or <div> to apply NUI button styles and behaviors.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the button behavior and appearance.

prefixIcon.description: Remix Icon class name shown before the content

suffixIcon.description: Remix Icon class name shown after the content

ariaLabel.description: Accessibility label (automatically calculated for icon-only buttons)

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the Button component.

onClick.description: Emitted when the button is clicked (not emitted if disabled or loading)

computed.title: Computed Properties
computed.description: Reactive computed properties (Signals) of the component.

effectiveColor.description: Resolved color (Input > Global config > Default value)

effectiveSize.description: Resolved size (Input > Global config > Default value)

effectiveVariant.description: Resolved variant (Input > Global config > Default value)

hasVisibleContent.description: True if the button has text content or label

isIconOnly.description: True if the button only contains an icon without text

models.title: Models
models.description: Data models related to the Button component.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variables to customize colors by variant (solid, outline, ghost) and color (primary, secondary, etc.).

solidHoverText.description: Text color on hover for solid

structure.title: Structural variables
structure.description: CSS variables for spacing, sizes, typography and visual effects.

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The Button resolves the accessible name automatically following a strict priority order. Always provide an explicit name for icon-only buttons.
naming.note: Icon-only buttons (isIconOnly) must have an aria-label or title input. Omitting both makes the button inaccessible.

textLabel.description: Projected text or the label input act as the accessible name without needing any additional attribute.

iconText.description: The icon automatically receives aria-hidden; the visible text is sufficient as the name.

iconOnly.description: One of the two is required. Priority: aria-label input > title input. The component exposes computedAriaLabel() with the resolved logic.

iconOnlyLoading.description: The spinner visually replaces the icon but the accessible name persists while the button remains in the DOM.

states.title: ARIA attributes
states.description: The Button component adds and removes ARIA attributes automatically based on its state. Consumer code does not need to add any additional bindings.

ariaLabel.description: Resolved by computedAriaLabel(). Omitted when the button has visible text (the native name is sufficient).

ariaBusy.description: Present when loading=true. Indicates to ATs that the button is processing an operation.

disabled.description: Applied when disabled=true or when loading=true. Blocks focus and events natively, without needing aria-disabled.

ariaHidden.description: All icon <i> elements and spinner <span> elements carry aria-hidden so ATs do not read them.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The component uses a native <button> element, so the browser provides all keyboard behavior for free.
keyboard.note: When disabled or loading are true, the native disabled attribute is set, which automatically removes the button from the tab order.

tab.description: Moves focus to the button (or away from it). disabled / loading buttons are excluded from the tab order.

shiftTab.description: Moves focus to the previous element.

spaceEnter.description: Activates the button. If disabled or loading, the handler intercepts and cancels the event before onClick fires.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: Set default values for all Button instances in your application using the provideNUI() function. Any property defined here is automatically applied to every button that does not override it via a direct input binding.
setup.note: Only the properties you want to override need to be defined. Properties not specified fall back to the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: Full list of configurable properties through ButtonGlobalConfig, their types and the library defaults.

rows.color: Color theme applied to all buttons by default
rows.size: Size applied to all buttons by default
rows.variant: Visual variant applied to all buttons by default
rows.shape: Shape applied to all buttons by default
rows.raised: Whether buttons have a shadow by default
rows.width: Width behaviour applied to all buttons by default
rows.type: Native button type applied by default
rows.loadingPosition: Loading spinner position applied by default

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: Values are resolved following a strict three-level priority chain. The first matching level wins: direct input > global config > library default.
priority.note: The priority chain guarantees that components always have a valid resolved value. A direct input always wins; if not provided, the global config value is used; if not configured either, the library default is applied.
